source: camelid /v1/completions (logprobs=5) on the exact 1024-bucket prompt (Qwen3-0.6B-Q8_0, fully-GPU-resident CUDA decode, temp 0/top_k 1/seed 0) — same runtime that produced context-parity-sweep-512-8192.json. Captured to make the ~0.10-nat near-tie magnitude reader-verifiable rather than manifest-asserted (matching the qwen3-4b-q4km near-tie-2048-logprobs.json precedent).
interpretation: At generation index 12 (both engines already inside the tiny 0.6B's degenerate repetition loop) camelid's top token is 'The' (-1.40) and the llama.cpp oracle's pick 'Answer' is camelid's #2 by only ~0.10 nat. A benign greedy coin-flip between two degenerate continuations, not a camelid decode error; the adjacent longer buckets 2048/4096/8192 are token-identical, so this is an isolated flip, not a context-length failure. Because the token stream is not identical, the 1024 bucket is NOT promoted.
